cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the statements is true according to the passage. The question is about the writer's experience with their memory. So, the writer thinks they locked the door, but actually didn't. Looking at the options: A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. B) One night the writer forgot having locked the front door. C) The writer remembered to lock the door. D) The writer remembered unlocking the front door. This is a bit confusing. If they forgot having locked it, that would mean they didn't remember that they had locked it. However, the passage says they clearly remember locking it. But the door was actually unlocked. So maybe they mistakenly remembered locking it when they didn't. So B is about forgetting that they had locked it, but the passage says they remembered locking it, but in reality, they didn't do it.
